lines.en_lit: And does he burn his flock? Is it comparable? There, it is not written: “It,” here, it is written: “It,”

It was stated Rav Pappi said in the name of Rava: He sends the first and Rav Shimi said in the name of Rava: He sends the last Granted, Rav Shimi bar Ashi in the name of Rava, who said he sends the last he holds since atonement is completed with it. However, Rav Pappi in the name of Rava, what does he hold? He holds in accordance with Rabbi Yosei, mitzva is with the first

which Rabbi Yosei If we say Rabbi Yosei containers, As we learned Rabbi Yosei says: three containers of three se’a each, with which they collect the donation from the chamber. And alef, beit, gimmel were written on the
And it was taught Rabbi Yosei said: Why were alef, beit, gimmel written on them? to ascertain which of them the donation was collected first, bring from it first, as mitzva is of the first
perhaps at the time when the first was fit to be used, the last was not fit to be used.

Rather, Rabbi Yosei with regard to the Paschal offering, as it was taught one who separated his Paschal offering and was lost, and he separated another in its stead, and afterward the first was found, and they both stand he sacrifices whichever of them he wants; the statement of the Rabbis. Rabbi Yosei says: mitzva with the first And if the second was superior to it, he brings

MISHNA: The mitzva of the two Yom Kippur goats, is That they will both be identical in appearance, and in height, and in monetary value, and their acquisition as one, And even if they are not identical, they are valid. he acquired one today and one tomorrow, they are valid.
one died, if it died before drew the lots, he takes a counterpart for the second, And if it died after drew the lots, he brings another pair and draws lots over them from the start.

he utters If to God died, that upon which the lot for God shall stand in its stead; and if to Azazel died, that upon which the lot for Azazel shall stand in its stead. And the second, should graze until it becomes unfit, it will be sold, and the money its will go toward a gift-offering. as a communal sin-offering not to die. Rabbi Yehuda says: it should die.
And furthermore, Rabbi Yehuda said: the blood spilled the scapegoat to die. if the scapegoat dies, the blood should be spilled,
